threats.title: SQL Injection Vulnerability
threats.description: Attackers inject malicious SQL code into input fields (e.g., login forms, search boxes) to manipulate database queries, potentially gaining unauthorized access to data, modifying records, or even executing arbitrary commands on the database server.
threats.attack_vector: Unvalidated user input used directly in SQL queries.
mitigations: Utilize parameterized queries or prepared statements to prevent the interpretation of user input as SQL code.
Implement strict input validation and sanitization to filter out potentially malicious characters.
Employ a Web Application Firewall (WAF) to detect and block SQL injection attempts.

threats.title: Credential Stuffing Attack
threats.description: Attackers utilize lists of compromised usernames and passwords (credential stuffing) to attempt login to the system. This leverages existing data breaches to gain unauthorized access.
threats.attack_vector: Brute-force or dictionary attacks against username/password combinations obtained from external breaches.
mitigations: Implement Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) to significantly reduce the impact of compromised credentials.
Rate limiting login attempts to mitigate brute-force attacks.
Regularly monitor for unusual login activity and investigate suspicious accounts.

threats.title: Resource Exhaustion via Malicious Input
threats.description: Attackers submit large or complex requests to the system, overwhelming its resources (e.g., CPU, memory, bandwidth) and causing a denial of service.
threats.attack_vector: Malformed requests, excessive data uploads, or computationally intensive operations.
mitigations: Implement rate limiting to restrict the number of requests that a user or IP address can make within a given time period.
Validate and sanitize user input to prevent the submission of malicious or oversized requests.
Utilize a Content Delivery Network (CDN) to distribute traffic and reduce the load on the origin server.

threats.title: IDOR (Insecure Direct Object Reference)
threats.description: Attackers exploit vulnerabilities in the application's authorization mechanisms to access resources or perform actions that they are not authorized to perform by manipulating identifiers (e.g., user IDs, object IDs) in URLs or API requests.
threats.attack_vector: Lack of proper authorization checks when accessing resources based on user-supplied identifiers.
mitigations: Implement robust authorization controls to verify that users have the necessary permissions to access specific resources.
Avoid using direct object references in URLs or API requests; instead, use indirect references or tokens.
Regularly review and test authorization mechanisms to identify and address potential vulnerabilities.

threats.title: Exposure of Internal System Information via Logs
threats.description: Insufficiently sanitized or secured log files expose sensitive internal system information (e.g., database connection strings, internal IP addresses, API keys) to unauthorized access, potentially enabling attackers to compromise the system or gain access to other systems on the network.
threats.attack_vector: Direct access to log files without proper security controls or encryption.
mitigations: Implement strict access controls to log files, limiting access to authorized personnel only.
Encrypt log files to protect sensitive information in transit and at rest.
Regularly review and audit log files for any sensitive information that may have been inadvertently exposed.

mapped_threats.title: SQL Injection Vulnerability
mapped_threats.description: Attackers inject malicious SQL code into input fields (e.g., login forms, search boxes) to manipulate database queries, potentially gaining unauthorized access to data, modifying records, or even executing arbitrary commands on the database server.
mapped_threats.attack_vector: Unvalidated user input used directly in SQL queries.
mitigations: Utilize parameterized queries or prepared statements to prevent the interpretation of user input as SQL code.
Implement strict input validation and sanitization to filter out potentially malicious characters.
Employ a Web Application Firewall (WAF) to detect and block SQL injection attempts.

mapped_threats.title: Exposure of Internal System Information via Logs
mapped_threats.description: Insufficiently sanitized or secured log files expose sensitive internal system information (e.g., database connection strings, internal IP addresses, API keys) to unauthorized access, potentially enabling attackers to compromise the system or gain access to other systems on the network.
mapped_threats.attack_vector: Direct access to log files without proper security controls or encryption.

Encrypt log files to protect sensitive information in transit and at rest.
Regularly review and audit log files for any sensitive information that may have been inadvertently exposed.

gaps: Potential gaps in logging and monitoring for information disclosure (SC-13, SC-28, SC-8)
Lack of specific controls addressing complex attack vectors beyond those covered by the listed controls.
priorities: High - Credential Stuffing and SQL Injection (Severity: High) - Immediate remediation required.
Medium - IDOR and Resource Exhaustion (Severity: High) - Requires rapid assessment and mitigation.

dfd_mermaid:
flowchart TB

    %% Styling
    classDef external fill:#e1f5fe,stroke:#0277bd,stroke-width:2px
    classDef process fill:#e8f5e9,stroke:#2e7d32,stroke-width:2px
    classDef datastore fill:#fff3e0,stroke:#ef6c00,stroke-width:2px
    classDef threat fill:#ffebee,stroke:#c62828,stroke-width:3px

    subgraph External["External Entities"]
        user[("👤 User")]
        admin[("👔 Admin")]
        external_api[("🌐 External API")]
    end

    subgraph TrustBoundary["Trust Boundary"]
        subgraph Frontend["Frontend Layer"]
            web_app["🖥️ Web Application"]
        end

        subgraph Backend["Backend Layer"]
            api_gateway["🚪 API Gateway"]
            auth_service["🔐 Auth Service"]
            core_api["⚙️ Core API"]
        end

        subgraph Data["Data Layer"]
            database[("💾 Database")]
            cache[("⚡ Cache")]
        end
    end

    %% Data Flows
    user -->|"🔒 HTTPS"| web_app
    admin -->|"🔒 HTTPS + MFA"| web_app
    web_app -->|"🔒 API Calls"| api_gateway
    api_gateway -->|"🔒 Auth Check"| auth_service
    api_gateway -->|"🔒 Business Logic"| core_api
    auth_service -->|"🔒 User Data"| database
    core_api -->|"🔒 CRUD"| database
    core_api -->|"⚠️ Session"| cache
    core_api -->|"🔒 External Call"| external_api

    %% Apply Styles
    class user,admin,external_api external
    class web_app,api_gateway,auth_service,core_api process
    class database,cache datastore
    class web_app,api_gateway,auth_service,core_api,database threat

semgrep.config_file:
rules:
- fix: cursor.execute($QUERY, (params,))
  id: security-custom-1
  languages:
  - python
  - javascript
  - typescript
  message: SQL Injection Vulnerability
  metadata:
    category: security
    confidence: HIGH
    cwe: CWE-89
    owasp: A03:2021
    threat_category: Tampering
  patterns:
  - pattern: cursor.execute($QUERY)
    pattern-not: cursor.execute($QUERY, $PARAMS)
  severity: ERROR
- id: security-custom-2
  languages:
  - python
  - javascript
  - typescript
  message: Credential Stuffing Attack
  metadata:
    category: security
    confidence: HIGH
    cwe: CWE-287
    owasp: A07:2021
    threat_category: Spoofing
  patterns:
  - pattern: password = "..."
  - pattern: secret = "..."
  - pattern: api_key = "..."
  severity: ERROR
- id: security-custom-3
  languages:
  - python
  - javascript
  - typescript
  message: Resource Exhaustion via Malicious Input
  metadata:
    category: security
    confidence: HIGH
    cwe: CWE-400
    owasp: A05:2021
    threat_category: Denial of Service
  patterns:
  - pattern: eval($X)
  - pattern: exec($X)
  severity: ERROR
- id: security-custom-4
  languages:
  - python
  - javascript
  - typescript
  message: IDOR (Insecure Direct Object Reference)
  metadata:
    category: security
    confidence: HIGH
    cwe: CWE-269
    owasp: A01:2021
    threat_category: Elevation of Privilege
  patterns:
  - pattern: eval($X)
  - pattern: exec($X)
  severity: ERROR
- id: security-custom-5
  languages:
  - python
  - javascript
  - typescript
  message: Exposure of Internal System Information via Logs
  metadata:
    category: security
    confidence: HIGH
    cwe: CWE-200
    owasp: A01:2021
    threat_category: Information Disclosure
  patterns:
  - pattern: print($SENSITIVE)
  - pattern: console.log($SENSITIVE)
  severity: ERROR
